---
title: Testing the pipeline
subtitle: For contributors changing the bp-submission-pipeline
---

This page is about testing the **`bp-submission-pipeline`** itself — not this
documentation site. If you're editing manual pages, see
[Page template & conventions](page-template.qmd) instead.

The pipeline ships two developer test targets. Run them before opening a PR to
catch breakage early:

| Target | Cost | Run it when |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `make test-unit` | Fast, no Docker (~2 sec) | **Every** change, always before a PR |
| `make smoke` | Heavy, boots the full Docker stack | You've touched pipeline **behavior** (API client, submitter, export, compose/env wiring) |

## `make test-unit`

**What it covers.** Unit and contract tests — the seams between the pipeline and
its submodules (env vars, directory layout, the XLSX/mapper contracts, the API
client, the submitter argv, credential generation). It runs `pytest tests/unit/`
and needs **no Docker** and no running stack.

**When to run.** On every change, and always before opening a PR. It's cheap, so
there's no reason to skip it.

**Prerequisites.** A Python with `pytest` importable. The target invokes
`python3 -m pytest`, so you don't need a `pytest` shim on your `PATH` — but you
do need `pytest` installed in that interpreter (`python3 -m pip install pytest`).
If `make` can't find `python3` on your machine, run the underlying command
directly instead:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=bigpicture-metadata-interface:bp-submitter python -m pytest tests/unit/ -v
```

**What a pass looks like.** A green pytest summary (your counts and timing will
differ):

```
tests/unit/test_env_contract.py::test_required_vars_present PASSED
tests/unit/test_xlsx_contract.py::test_columns_match_model PASSED
...
============================ 211 passed in 2.13s ============================
```

**What a failure looks like.** pytest names the failing test and the assertion;
the run ends with a red summary:

```
tests/unit/test_xlsx_contract.py::test_columns_match_model FAILED
...
E   AssertionError: column 'specimen_type' missing from model.json
======================= 1 failed, 210 passed in 2.1s =======================
```

Fix the contract that broke (or update the test if the contract changed
deliberately) and re-run until green.

## `make smoke`

**What it checks.** The full end-to-end path against a metadata XLSX: it
generates test credentials, boots the minimal stack (`pipeline-db`,
`slidetap-app`, `slidetap-task`), waits for the API to become healthy, then
drives a complete submission via the REST API — create project, upload the XLSX,
image pre- and post-processing, complete the batch, export the bundle, verify the
outbox, and finally run `bp-submitter --dry-run` over the exported dataset. If
all of that succeeds, the submit→export→upload contract is intact.

**When to run.** When your change touches pipeline behavior — the API client,
the submitter, export logic, or the Docker Compose / `.env` wiring. It's slow
(image processing does real DICOM conversion and can take minutes per slide), so
it's not worth running for pure docs or typo changes.

**Prerequisites.** Unlike `test-unit`, smoke needs a working environment: a
configured `.env`, Docker running, and credentials in place. If you haven't set
that up, follow [D. Configure](../configure.qmd) and
[G. Preflight + boot](../boot.qmd) first.

By default smoke uses the bundled fixture `tests/data/metadata/metadata.xlsx`.
To run against your own metadata, pass `XLSX`:

```bash
make smoke XLSX=path/to/your/metadata.xlsx
```

**What a pass looks like.** Progress is logged stage by stage, ending with
`Smoke finished`:

```
API healthy
Logging in as admin...
Creating project...
Waiting for metadata import to complete (METADATA_SEARCH_COMPLETE)...
  status=3
Metadata import complete
...
Export ready
Running bp-submitter --dry-run on project smoke-project-...
Smoke finished
```

**What a failure looks like.** The run aborts at the stage that broke. The two
most common early failures:

```
Error: .env not found. Copy .env.example to .env and update values.
```

```
Missing XLSX: tests/data/metadata/metadata.xlsx
```

(both exit with code 2 before anything boots), and — once the stack is starting —
the API health timeout:

```
Error: API did not become healthy within 60 seconds
```

which usually means a container failed to start; check `docker compose logs` for
the offending service.
